retry_reason: Failure (NULL return) indicates no matching socket or a kernel-internal inconsistency; retrying cannot fix a non-existent match or a WARN condition.
contract_summary: Get-wrapper that looks up a socket identified by the tuple and returns it with an acquired reference. If the lookup returns a request_sock, the function releases the request_sock reference and returns the full socket (listener) without incrementing its reference. The caller must release the returned socket with sock_put (or equivalent) when done. The function returns NULL if no socket is found or if an unreferenced non-RCU socket is encountered (kernel bug).

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to acquire a reference-counted socket (by calling bpf_skc_lookup) and return it to the caller; the additional logic to handle request_sock conversion and conditional put is still strictly reference-count management, with no unrelated business logic.
judge.retry_reason: Failure (NULL return) indicates no matching socket or a kernel-internal inconsistency; retrying cannot fix a non-existent match or a WARN condition.
judge.contract_summary: Get-wrapper that looks up a socket identified by the tuple and returns it with an acquired reference. If the lookup returns a request_sock, the function releases the request_sock reference and returns the full socket (listener) without incrementing its reference. The caller must release the returned socket with sock_put (or equivalent) when done. The function returns NULL if no socket is found or if an unreferenced non-RCU socket is encountered (kernel bug).

check.reason: The candidate operation indicates a 'put' on the return value, but the function does not decrement the refcount of the returned socket. The only 'put' is via sock_gen_put(sk) on the local variable sk before it may be reassigned, which is not the return value. The return value retains a reference (from bpf_skc_lookup or sk_to_full_sk without additional put). Thus, the extracted operation is inconsistent with the code.

reevaluate.reason: The function bpf_sk_lookup returns a socket with a reference obtained from bpf_skc_lookup, but it does not explicitly increment the refcount of the returned socket. The only explicit refcount operation is sock_gen_put(sk) on the local variable sk (the original lookup result) when it is replaced by sk_to_full_sk(sk). This put is not on the returned socket. Therefore, the function does not have explicit refcount operations on the critical variable passed to the caller, and it is not a reference-count wrapper/helper as defined by the analysis.
